texto: She had to lift both hands to illustrate what she meant, but he just let her carry his hand with her, not about to let go. She pushed the free hand toward the one he held, apparently trying to gesture closeness. "Warm," she said again. And then she did something that undid him to the last faint whisper of his soul: she gave his hand a squeeze with fingertips that could just barely reach around his, apparently using him to indicate what she wanted to say. He meant warmth. He meant this word she couldn't find.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Florand sobre temas ligados a amor. “She had to lift both hands to illustrate what she meant, but he just let her carry his hand with …” articula amor, chocolate, paris em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como paris e romance.

frase_original: She had to lift both hands to illustrate what she meant, but he just let her carry his hand with her, not about to let go. She pushed the free hand toward the one he held, apparently trying to gesture closeness. "Warm," she said again. And then she did something that undid him to the last faint whisper of his soul: she gave his hand a squeeze with fingertips that could just barely reach around his, apparently using him to indicate what she wanted to say. He meant warmth. He meant this word she couldn't find.

texto: A man's strength was supposed to be against the outside world: to fight it back from himself and those he took under his protection: his wife, his children, and for a man strong enough, more people still, people like his employees. To turn it inward, against the very people you had been given that strength to protect, because you couldn't deal with the outward fight, was the ultimate weakness.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Laura Florand: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “A man's strength was supposed to be against the outside world: to fight it back from himself and …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, abuse, strength pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de strength.

texto: You can't ever really have enough worth that when you're gone, the spot you left won't close over.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Florand sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “You can't ever really have enough worth that when you're gone, the spot you left won't close over.” articula autoestima, pessimism, solitude em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como solitude e worth.
